endpoints.environment_bound: full-dataset Docker batching belongs on a native-x86 CI runner with cached images - the standard SWE-bench execution environment - not local arm64 emulation; this is a bound on LOCAL execution, not on the method, which iter132 proved works
environment_constraints: concurrent multi-GB x86 image pulls under arm64 emulation strained the Docker daemon; an already-cached image (13480) returned a spurious PULL_FAIL under load
a serial run of a second instance (13615) completed with exit 0 but produced no captured container output - flaky stdout under emulation
endpoints.finding: the Docker harness batches by construction (each instance is an independent proven image run), but local batch execution is unreliable under arm64 emulation - multi-GB pull cost, daemon load, flaky emulated-container stdout; full-dataset batching is a native-x86 CI operation, not open research

single_instance_reference.result: base fails with real bug, gold passes, pinned Python 3.9
